cause: SL plan order was set (8% from entry) but liquidation happened before SL executed
root_cause: 8% SL on 10x leverage = only 2% buffer to liquidation price
SL plan order is a TRIGGER order not guaranteed stop — execution takes time
In fast market (price spiked in 20-second monitoring gap), liquidation engine faster than plan order execution
Price gapped from $1.9277 to past $1.989 (liq price) in under 20 seconds
Monitor interval was 20s — too slow to catch rapid spike
fix: Max SL: 3% (not 8%) — buffer to liquidation now 7%
Bot-side HARD STOP at -3%: force close WITHOUT waiting for plan order
Position size: 20% of balance (not 90%) — even full liquidation = 80% balance survives
Layer protection: bot-side → plan order → liquidation (3 layers)

cause: placePlanOrder only handled checkScale (size precision) but NOT checkBDScale (price precision)
example: CRCLUSDT TP price $70.024 rejected — checkBDScale=2 requires 2 decimal places → $70.02
fix: Parse checkBDScale from error message, round triggerPrice to required decimal places, retry
code_fix: const priceMatch = msg.match(/checkBDScale.*?checkScale=(\d+)/i); if (priceMatch) { tryPrice = round(triggerPrice, scale); continue; }
rule: ALWAYS retry plan orders with both size AND price scale fixes

cause: 90% position size with 10x leverage = 9x portfolio exposure
math: Balance $7.51 × 90% margin × 10x = $67.6 notional. One 10% adverse move = LIQUIDATED
fix: Max 20% position size. Even full liquidation = lose only 20% of balance
rule: POSITION_PCT = 0.20. Never exceed 30% regardless of confidence
lesson: High conviction ≠ high size. Risk management exists BECAUSE we can be wrong

cause: Main order filled at size 37.2 but plan order retried with size 37 (checkScale=0 for plan order)
consequence: 0.2 contracts remained unprotected after SL triggered
fix: After position confirmed open, use ACTUAL position size from API (all-position endpoint) for plan orders, not calculated size
rule: ALWAYS fetch actual position size from Bitget before placing plan orders

cause: 100% reliance on Bitget plan orders for SL — no backup mechanism
fix: 3 layers: (1) bot-side force close at -3%, (2) Bitget plan order SL, (3) design so liquidation != total loss
principle: DEFENSE IN DEPTH: No single point of failure. Each layer protects against the next layer failing

cause: RSI 42/58 threshold too strict — very few signals in ranging markets
fix: RSI 44/56 with Fib zone 25-75% — more signals while still avoiding extreme overbought/oversold entries
rule: In low-volatility markets, loosen RSI slightly. In high-volatility, tighten

cause: Only 30 pairs scanned, $5M min volume — misses good setups in mid-cap pairs
fix: 50 pairs, $2M min volume — 67% more pairs without significantly increasing noise
rule: Signal quality > signal quantity, but must have enough universe to find quality

cause: SL percentage set based on price movement, not validated against liquidation price

math.formula: MAX_SL_PCT = (100/LEVERAGE) - SAFETY_BUFFER  # 10x → (10-3=7% max, use 3% for safety)

rule: VALIDATE: assert slPrice < liquidationPrice - 2% buffer BEFORE placing plan order
future_implementation: Calculate liquidation price from Bitget API and validate SL is 5% away minimum
